gate: official legal-document chain and version identity
evidence: REF003-R01B (Platform v15 PDF, 42pp, docLink 8f54e...), REF003-R03B (legal-terms listing: Platform version 15 docLink 8f54e9463ad3fa8ab9c0a39c0fc25f3a.pdf, updateTime 2026-07-15)
notes: Platform Terms v15 PDF (400739B, 42pp, Last-Modified 2026-07-15) docLink identity 8f54e9463ad3fa8ab9c0a39c0fc25f3a.pdf matches the official legal-terms listing entry (name Bybit-BTL-Platform-Terms-and-Conditions, version 15, updateTime 2026-07-15). API Terms v1 is a distinct document (7pp, Last-Modified 2025-09-08, internal Title 'EN - API Terms & Conditions') not enumerated in the listing. Version identity established. Retrieval URLs not retained in headers (fail closed on lineage only). PASS.

gate: API Terms applicability versus Platform Terms
evidence: REF003-R02B (APIA s1.1 'this APIA will apply ... in addition to our standard Terms of Use'; s1.2 APIA prevails on inconsistency), REF003-R01B (Platform TOU)
notes: API Terms (APIA) apply 'in addition to' the Platform Terms of Use and prevail on inconsistency. The two documents are distinct and cumulative. APIA governs API use; Platform TOU governs Site/Platform/data. Applicability distinguished. PASS.

evidence: REF003-R02B (APIA s5.1: revocable license 'to use the API'; s2.2 'access, call, command, query or request the API')
notes: API Terms grant a limited, non-exclusive, non-sublicensable, non-transferable, non-assignable and revocable license to use the API, including automated query/request. Automated public-API acquisition for market data is within the granted license. PASS (subject to the documented s6.7/s6.9 restrictions, which the proposed scope does not rely on).

gate: permission for internal non-commercial raw snapshot retention
evidence: REF003-R01B/R02B (no retention grant); bybit 7-year clause is Bybit's own obligation
notes: Neither Platform Terms nor API Terms grants the user an explicit right to retain raw snapshots internally for non-commercial research. The 7-year retention clause is Bybit's own data-retention obligation, not a user permission. No explicit grant => fail-closed. FAIL-UNKNOWN.

gate: deterministic instruments-info pagination and request identity
evidence: REF003-R03B (legal-terms listing deterministic, ret_code 0); no instruments-info capture
notes: The legal-terms listing API is deterministic (ret_code 0, request identity retained). However, no instruments-info (v5/market) request/response was captured in this audit; pagination/request identity for instruments-info is unverified. FAIL-UNKNOWN.

evidence: methodology: captures stamped retrieved_at 2026-07-21; no backdating
notes: Audit commits to prospective semantics: each snapshot records known_from = retrieved_at only; knowledge is never backdated to a historical state. Self-imposed methodology constraint, within scope (historical transitions explicitly out of scope). PASS.

evidence: REF003-R01B (Platform bars 'commercial use of the Site or Platform' and 'scraping'); REF003-R02B (APIA s6.7 no repackage/resell Service Data; s6.9 no commercial exploitation)
notes: G07 asks whether the PROPOSED SCOPE assumes redistribution/commercial rights. The prospective scope (internal non-commercial raw snapshot retention, no redistribution) does NOT assume those rights; it explicitly avoids them. A documented provider prohibition alone is not a gate failure. The documents' s6.7/s6.9 restrictions are recorded as constraints on any future use, but do not fail this gate. PASS.

evidence: REF003-R01B/R02B PDFs with SHA-256 + versions + docLinks/filenames; REF003-R03B listing
notes: G08 is evidence-lineage only. Immutable snapshot/version lineage is established for both PDFs (SHA-256, version, docLink/filename identity) and the legal-terms listing (retained body+headers, ret_code 0). G08 must not fail because of redistribution terms. Lineage complete and reproducible from retained artifacts. PASS. (Retrieval URLs not retained in headers is a lineage limitation noted, not a redistribution failure.)
